steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: An effective introduction must convey both who Keating was and what he is known for: pioneering radio technology that led to the first transmission of speech by radio.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Harold Keating, an electronic communications specialist, worked at the Edison Machine Works manufacturing company.: Identifies Keating as an electronic communications specialist who worked for Edison, but omits his defining achievement — the radio transmission breakthrough. ✗
  • Choice B — Harold Keating, an electronic communications specialist and former employee of Thomas Edison, is known for pioneering radio technology that led to the first transmission of speech by radio.: Names his background (electronic communications specialist, Edison connection) and his achievement (pioneering radio technology that led to the first transmission of speech by radio). Both required elements are present. ✓
  • Choice C — Having received little formal education, Thomas Edison depended on specialized employees, including Harold Keating and Walter J. Ames, for many of his inventions.: Shifts focus to Edison and Ames rather than Keating's own contribution. ✗
  • Choice D — Thomas Edison's historic success would not have been possible without naval officer Walter J. Ames, who is known for being the father of electric traction.: Also centers Edison and Ames, leaving Keating's radio achievement unmentioned. ✗
That matches Choice B — Harold Keating, an electronic communications specialist and former employee of Thomas Edison, is known for pioneering radio technology that led to the first transmission of speech by radio..
